userdoc.notice: Stores encrypted medical record IPFS hashes on-chain and enforces         patient-controlled, time-limited consent before allowing access. CHANGES FROM v1:  - Interface updated to use hasValidConsent() — now checks expiry too  - Record versioning: each patient has a version counter; updates append    a new record rather than overwrite (immutable audit trail)  - RecordAccessed event now includes the accessor's role string  - getRecordsByPatient() added for frontend pagination  - nonReentrant guard on all write functions  - viewMedicalRecord split: returns hash + emits access log

params.ipfsHash: IPFS CID of the AES-256 encrypted record

params.accessorRole: Human-readable role label (passed by caller for the audit log)

returns.ipfsHash: IPFS CID of the encrypted record

addMedicalRecord(address,string,string).notice: Add a medical record for a patient.         Caller must hold valid patient consent.

getLatestVersion(address).notice: Returns the latest version number of a patient's records.         Useful for checking if a patient's records have been updated.

getRecordCount(address).notice: Returns the total number of records for a patient.

getRecordsByPatient(address).notice: Returns all record IDs belonging to a patient.         Frontend uses this to paginate a patient's record history.

viewMedicalRecord(uint256,string).notice: View a specific medical record and emit an access log.         Requires valid patient consent.

documentation.text:
@title  MedicalRecordsAndAccess
 @notice Stores encrypted medical record IPFS hashes on-chain and enforces
         patient-controlled, time-limited consent before allowing access.
 CHANGES FROM v1:
  - Interface updated to use hasValidConsent() — now checks expiry too
  - Record versioning: each patient has a version counter; updates append
    a new record rather than overwrite (immutable audit trail)
  - RecordAccessed event now includes the accessor's role string
  - getRecordsByPatient() added for frontend pagination
  - nonReentrant guard on all write functions
  - viewMedicalRecord split: returns hash + emits access log
@notice Returns the total number of records for a patient.

@notice Returns the latest version number of a patient's records.
         Useful for checking if a patient's records have been updated.
